description_long: This stone church features a rectangular nave with a steeply pitched, shingled roof and a prominent central steeple topped with a cross. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn gray stone, giving the building a robust and historic appearance. The front facade is marked by a set of steps leading to a main entrance, flanked by small, arched windows and a circular window above. Along the sides, a series of tall, narrow windows provide light to the interior, while the rear of the building mirrors the front but with fewer decorative elements. The steeple rises above the entrance, incorporating a bell tower with arched openings. The overall design is simple yet imposing, evoking traditional European church architecture.
description: A robust stone church with a steep shingled roof, central steeple, and arched windows, featuring a cross atop the spire and a prominent bell tower.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with steps, arched windows, circular window, and the central steeple above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long stone wall, series of tall narrow windows, and the full height of the steeple and bell tower.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler stone facade, fewer windows, and the cross-topped steeple visible above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the nave, stonework, and the bell tower structure.
